revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]・[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]]今福村が開発された際に創建したと伝わる。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年（）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。 また境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000690.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]・[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]]今福村が開発された際に創建したと伝わる。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。 また境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。同じく境内社の小女郎稲荷社は、関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000690.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]]今福村が開発された際に創建したと伝わる。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の小女郎稲荷社は、[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000690.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]]今福村が開発された際に創建したと伝わる。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-08-15}}。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の小女郎稲荷社は、[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる。神仏分離令までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で１９１１年(明治４４年)会計指定、翌１９１２年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、１９４７年(昭和２２年)、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-08-15}}。[[1995年|１９９５年]](平成７年)の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]にて拝殿一部が倒壊したため、翌年に拝殿屋根を瓦葺から銅板葺に改修した。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては鯰江村布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社　[[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる。神仏分離令までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で１９１１年(明治４４年)会計指定、翌１９１２年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、１９４７年(昭和２２年)、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-08-15}}。[[1995年|１９９５年]](平成７年)の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]にて拝殿一部が倒壊したため、翌年に拝殿屋根を瓦葺から銅板葺に改修した。 [[木下延重|木下與右衛門御検地帳]]（文禄3年）に東西二十六間南北十七間面積一反四畝四町歩を除地されたと記載されている{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 拝殿南西角に在る道標は１８３３年（天保３年）年３月に世話方６名により建立されたもの。もとは古堤街道（大和街道）の四辻の東南角に在った道標で、古堤街道は野崎詣、大峰山詣、伊勢詣の交通の要路であったことから建てられた。「中央・四辻の東のあたりで[[お蔭参り|伊勢の御札降り]]があり村民が踊り狂ったという伝承があり、のちに境内に持ち運ばれたと伝えられています。 １８５９年（安政六年）三月には今福村の大峰山登山人等により上部に常夜灯が加えられました。 道標には、「左　大阪道、右　のさき、ならいせ道　當村山上講」と刻まれています。 大正期まで樹齢千年以上の榎木の老大樹の御神木があった。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては鯰江村布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社　[[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる。神仏分離令までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で１９１１年(明治４４年)会計指定、翌１９１２年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、１９４７年(昭和２２年)、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた。{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-08-15}}。[[1995年|１９９５年]](平成７年)の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]にて拝殿一部が倒壊したため、翌年に拝殿屋根を瓦葺から銅板葺に改修した。 [[木下延重|木下與右衛門御検地帳]]（文禄3年）に東西二十六間南北十七間面積一反四畝四町歩を除地されたと記載されている{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 拝殿南西角に在る道標は１８３３年（天保３年）年３月に世話方６名により建立されたもの。もとは古堤街道（大和街道）の四辻の東南角に在った道標で、古堤街道は野崎詣、大峰山詣、伊勢詣の交通の要路であったことから建てられた。中央・四辻の東のあたりで[[お蔭参り|伊勢の御札降り]]があり村民が踊り狂ったという伝承があり、のちに境内に持ち運ばれたと伝えられています。のち１８５９年（安政六年）３月に今福村の大峰山登山人等により上部に常夜灯が加えられた。道標には、「左　大阪道、右　のさき、ならいせ道　當村山上講」と刻まれている。 大正期まで樹齢千年以上の榎木の老大樹の御神木があった。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては鯰江村布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社　[[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては[[鯰江町|鯰江村]]布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社　[[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 |祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 今福えびす
今福皇大神宮 |例祭 = [[4月22日]]
[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる。[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で１９１１年(明治４４年)会計指定、翌１９１２年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、１９４７年(昭和２２年)、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた。{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}。 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪府神社庁|accessdate=2024-08-15}}。[[1995年|１９９５年]](平成７年)の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]にて拝殿一部が倒壊したため、翌年に拝殿屋根を瓦葺から銅板葺に改修した。 [[木下延重|木下與右衛門御検地帳]]（[[1863年|文禄3年]]）に東西二十六間南北十七間面積一反四畝四町歩を除地されたと記載されている{{Cite web |title=由緒 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |website=koutaijingu |access-date=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=城東区史 |date=7月20日 |year=昭和49年 |publisher=城東区史編纂委員会}}。 拝殿南西角に在る道標は[[1833年|１８３３年]]（[[天保]]３年）年３月に世話方６名により建立されたもの。もとは古堤街道（大和街道）の四辻の東南角に在った道標で、古堤街道は野崎詣、大峰山詣、伊勢詣の交通の要路であったことから建てられた。中央・四辻の東のあたりで[[お蔭参り|伊勢の御札降り]]があり村民が踊り狂ったという伝承があり、のちに境内に持ち運ばれたと伝えられている。{{Cite book|和書 |title=城東区史 |date=7月20日 |publisher=城東区史編纂委員会 |year=昭和49年}}のち[[1859年|１８５９年]]（[[安政]]六年）３月に今福村の大峰山登山人等により上部に常夜灯が加えられた。道標には、「左　大阪道、右　のさき、ならいせ道　當村山上講」と刻まれている。 [[大正]]期まで樹齢千年以上の榎木の老大樹の御神木があった。{{Cite book|和書 |title=本多林学博士著 「大日本老樹名木誌」 |year=大正2年 |publisher=大日本山林会}} 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては[[鯰江町|鯰江村]]布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社　[[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠 == 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所 == 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite 皇大神宮] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんくう}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:城東区の建築物]] [[Category:城東区の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]]

[[10月22日]] |神事 = }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳{{cite web|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html|title=皇大神宮|publisher=大阪市|accessdate=2024-8-15}}。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる。[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で1911年（明治44年）会計指定、翌1912年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、1947年（昭和22年）、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた{{Cite 現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された{{Cite [[木下延重|木下與右衛門御検地帳]]（[[1863年]]〈文禄3年〉）に東西二十六間南北十七間面積一反四畝四町歩を除地されたと記載されている{{R|koutaijingu}}{{Cite book|和書 |editor=木村武夫 |title=城東区史 |date=1974年7月20日 |publisher=城東区史編纂委員会 |page={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。 拝殿南西角に在る道標は[[1833年]]（[[天保]]3年）3月に世話方6名により建立されたもの。もとは古堤街道（大和街道）の四辻の東南角に在った道標で、古堤街道は野崎詣、大峰山詣、伊勢詣の交通の要路であったことから建てられた。中央・四辻の東のあたりで[[お蔭参り|伊勢の御札降り]]があり村民が踊り狂ったという伝承があり、のちに境内に持ち運ばれたと伝えられている{{Cite book|和書 |editor=木村武夫 |title=城東区史 |date=1974年7月20日 |publisher=城東区史編纂委員会 |page={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。のち[[1859年]]（[[安政]]6年）3月に今福村の大峰山登山人等により上部に常夜灯が加えられた。道標には、「左　大阪道、右　のさき、ならいせ道　當村山上講」と刻まれている。 [[大正]]期まで樹齢千年以上の榎木の老大樹の御神木があった{{Cite book|和書 |editor=本多静六 |title=大日本老樹名木誌 |year=1913 |publisher=大日本山林会 |page={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。 境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている。 また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては[[鯰江町|鯰江村]]布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]] == 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * {{神社 | 名称 = 皇大神宮 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大阪市}} | 所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]2丁目12-31 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 41 | 緯度秒 = 51.7 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 3.1 | 祭神 = [[天照皇大御神]] | 社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = | 別名 = {{Ublist| 今福えびす | 今福皇大神宮 }} | 例祭 = {{Ublist| [[4月22日]] | [[10月22日]] }} | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Osaka city }} '''皇大神宮'''（こうたいじんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[城東区]][[今福南]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。「今福えびす」の愛称で親しまれる。
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳<ref name="大阪市">{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=大阪市 |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>。[[平安時代]]末期に[[摂津国]][[久岐今福御厨]]の開発された際に創建したと伝わる<ref name="大阪市" />。[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]までは[[三十番神]]社と称した。近代社格制度における社格は村社で1911年（[[明治]]44年）会計指定、翌1912年[[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛供進社]]となり、1947年（[[昭和]]22年）、[[神道指令]]により国家の管理より離れた<ref name="公式">{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |language=ja |access-date=2024-11-20}}</ref>。
現在の社殿は[[1742年]]（[[寛保]]2年）と[[1842年]]（[[天保]]13年）と[[1880年]]（明治13年）にそれぞれ改修され、更に[[1932年]]（昭和7年）に[[大阪城]]の設計を手がけた古川重春によって改築された<ref name="大阪市" /><ref>{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=大阪府神社庁 |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>。[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]にて拝殿一部が倒壊したため、翌年に拝殿屋根を瓦葺から銅板葺に改修した。
[[木下延重|木下與右衛門御検地帳]]（[[1863年]]（文禄3年））に東西二十六間南北十七間面積一反四畝四町歩を除地されたと記載されている<ref name="公式" />{{Sfn|木村|1971|p={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。
拝殿南西角に在る道標は[[1833年]]（天保3年）年3月に世話方6名により建立されたもの。もとは古堤街道（大和街道）の四辻の東南角に在った道標で、古堤街道は野崎詣、大峰山詣、伊勢詣の交通の要路であったことから建てられた。中央・四辻の東のあたりで[[お蔭参り|伊勢の御札降り]]があり村民が踊り狂ったという伝承があり、のちに境内に持ち運ばれたと伝えられている{{Sfn|木村|1971|p={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。のち[[1859年]]（[[安政]]6年）3月に今福村の大峰山登山人等により上部に常夜灯が加えられた。道標には、「左 大阪道、右 のさき、ならいせ道 當村山上講」と刻まれている。
[[大正]]期まで樹齢千年以上の榎木の老大樹の御神木があった{{Sfn|本多|1913|p={{要ページ番号|date=2024年11月}}}}。
境内にある今福恵比須社は「今福えびす」の愛称で知られ、毎年1月の恵比須市は[[第二次世界大戦]]前から催されている<ref name="大阪市" />。
また同じく境内社の[[小女郎稲荷社]]は、『'''[[摂津名所図会]]'''』第三巻にも記載される稲荷社。かつては[[鯰江町|鯰江村]]布屋新田の狐山に鎮座した。[[源九郎稲荷神社]]・[[信太森葛葉稲荷神社]]と並び関西三大稲荷のひとつに数えられている<ref name="大阪市" />。
== 祭神 == * [[天照皇大御神]]
== 境内社 == * 小女郎稲荷社 * 今福恵比須社 * 天満宮 * 天神社 - [[菅楯彦]]邸で祀られていた祠
== 境外社 == * 弁財天社 * 吉松彦七稲荷社 * 行宮所
== 交通 == * [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]]・[[Osaka Metro今里筋線|今里筋線]]「[[蒲生四丁目駅]]」より徒歩で約5分。 * [[阪神高速17号西大阪線]]「[[森之宮出入口]]」より車で約10分。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村武夫 |title=城東区史 |publisher=城東区史編纂委員会 |date=1971-07-20 |ncid=BN13899986 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1971}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=本多静六 |title=大日本老樹名木誌 |publisher=大日本山林会 |year=1913 |id={{NDLJP|951274}} |ref={{SfnRef|本多|1913}}}}
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|398836241}} * {{Official website|url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite|name=皇大神宮}} - 公式ウェブサイト * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪市 * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html 皇大神宮] - 大阪府神社庁

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Kōtai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami}}. The Main shrine is the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and shrines with the same name are Branch shrines of that shrine. Similar to shrines called {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮}}. While {{ill|Jingu|ja|神宮}} is a highly prestigious title limited to only a few shrines. Kotai Jingus and Daijingus..."
Removing from [[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Copying from [[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] to [[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Removing from [[Category:Shrines with identical names]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}
== References == <references />

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}
== References == <references />
== Sources == * {{Cite book |editor=Kimura Takeo |title=Joto Ward History |publisher=Joto Ward History Editorial Committee |date=1971-07-20 |ncid=BN13899986 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1971}}}} * {{Cite book |editor=Honda Seiroku |title=Great Japan Ancient Trees and Famous Trees |publisher=Dai Nippon Forestry Association |year=1913 |id={{NDLJP|951274}} |ref={{SfnRef|Honda|1913}}}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite|name=Kotai Jingū}} – Official website * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html Kotai Jingū] – Osaka City * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html Kotai Jingū] – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | image = {{image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | image = {{image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".

In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | image = {{image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".

The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | image = {{image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}

'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".

The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.

This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.

[[Category:Shimmei Shrines]] [[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Otheruses|{{ill|Kōtai Jingū (disambiguation)|ja|皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}}} [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]][[Category:Kotai Jingu disambiguation forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[en:Kōtaijingū (Osaka)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/皇大神宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotai Jingū (皇大神宮) | image = {{image requested|cat=Osaka city}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}} {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}} 2-chome 12-31 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 51.7 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 33 | Longitude seconds = 3.1 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = | Alias = {{Ublist| Imafuku Ebisu | Imafuku Kotai Jingū }} | Annual festival = {{Ublist| {{ill|April 22|ja|4月22日}} | {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} }} | Map = Japan Osaka city }}
'''Kotai Jingū''' (皇大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imafuku Minami|ja|今福南}}, {{ill|Joto Ward|ja|城東区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is affectionately known as "Imafuku Ebisu".
== History ==
The founding year is unknown<ref name="Osaka City">{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/joto/page/0000000740.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2024-08-15 |language=ja}}</ref>. It is said to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} when {{ill|Kuki Imafuku Mikuriya|ja|久岐今福御厨}} was developed<ref name="Osaka City" />. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} edict, it was known as the {{ill|Thirty Guardian Deities Shrine|ja|三十番神社}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated a village shrine, received accounting designation in 1911 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 44), and became a {{ill|Shinsen heihaku ryō kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛供進社}} in 1912. In 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 22), it was removed from state control under the {{ill|Shintō Directive|ja|神道指令}}<ref name="Official">{{Cite web |url=https://koutaijingu.wixsite.com/mysite/blank-1 |title=由緒 |publisher=皇大神宮 |accessdate=2024-11-20 |language=ja}}</ref>.
The current shrine building was renovated in 1742 ({{ill|Kanpō era|ja|寛保}} 2), 1842 ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 13), and 1880 (Meiji 13), and reconstructed by {{ill|Furukawa Shigeharu|ja|古川重春}}, who designed {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}, in 1932 (Shōwa 7)<ref name="Osaka City" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/joto-ku/04006kotaijingu.html |title=皇大神宮 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2024-08-15}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災}} of 1995 (Heisei 7), part of the worship hall collapsed. It was repaired the following year with a copper roof replacing the previous tile roof.
The {{ill|Kinoshita Yōemon Land Survey Record|ja|木下与右衛門御検地帳}} (1594, Bunroku 3) states that the shrine was exempted from taxation on an area measuring 26 ken east to west and 17 ken north to south (1 tan, 4 se, 4 chōbu)<ref name="Official" />{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The stone guidepost at the southwest corner of the worship hall was erected in March 1833 (Tenpō 3) by six caretakers. Originally, it stood at the southeastern corner of the crossroads on the {{ill|Kotsutsumi Kaidō (Old Embankment Road)|ja|古堤街道}} (Yamato Road), a major route for pilgrimages to {{ill|Nozaki|ja|野崎}}, {{ill|Ōmine Mountain|ja|大峰山}}, and {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. A local legend recounts that villagers danced wildly here during the {{ill|Okagemairi|ja|お蔭参り}} pilgrimage frenzy, and the guidepost was later moved to the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Kimura|1971|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}. In March 1859 ({{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政}} 6), a lantern was added to the top of the guidepost by pilgrims from Imafuku village heading to Mount Ōmine. The guidepost reads:
"Left: Osaka Road. Right: Nozaki, Nara, Ise Road. Tōsonzan Pilgrimage Group."
Until the {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} period, a sacred old enoki tree, over 1,000 years old, stood on the shrine grounds{{Sfn|Honda|1913|p={{page needed|date=November 2024}}}}.
The Imafuku Ebisu Shrine on the premises is known as "Imafuku Ebisu". The annual Ebisu Market held every January has continued since before {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
The shrine grounds also host the {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}}, which is mentioned in Volume 3 of the '''{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}}'''. This Inari shrine was originally located on {{ill|Kitsune-yama|ja|狐山}} in {{ill|Namazue Village|ja|鯰江村}}. It is considered one of the "Three Great Inari Shrines of Kansai", alongside {{ill|Genkurō Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shindōmori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社}}<ref name="Osaka City" />.
== deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大御神}}
== Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) == * {{ill|Kojirō Inari Shrine|ja|小女郎稲荷社}} * {{ill|Imafuku Ebisu Shrine|ja|今福恵比須社}} * {{ill|Tenman Shrine|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} – A shrine from the residence of artist {{ill|Sugawara Tatekiko|ja|菅楯彦}}
== External Shrines (境外社) == * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天社}} * {{ill|Yoshimatsu Hikosuke Inari Shrine|ja|吉松彦七稲荷社}} * {{ill|Gyōgūsho|ja|行宮所}}
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Gamo Yonchome Station|ja|蒲生四丁目駅}} on the {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} and {{ill|Osaka Metro Imazato Line|ja|Osaka Metro今里筋線}} * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Morinomiya Exit|ja|森之宮出入口}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 17 Nishi-Osaka Line|ja|阪神高速17号西大阪線}}
